title: `QBit` doesn't accept array literals of compatible types in `INSERT ... SELECT`
description:
### Problem
```sql
SET allow_experimental_qbit_type = 1;
CREATE TABLE test
(
`qbit` QBit(Float32, 3)
)
ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO test SELECT [1, 2, 3];
```
> Code: 53. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: Cannot convert from Array(UInt8) to QBit(Float32, 3): while converting source column `[1, 2, 3]` to destination column qbit. (TYPE_MISMATCH)
However, `INSERT INTO test SELECT [toFloat32(1), 2, 3];` would work. `UInt8` is compatible and should be casted to `Float32` for usability
### Stack Trace

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, qbit FLOAT8[]);
INSERT INTO test1 (id, qbit) SELECT number, ARRAY[to_float(1.0 * (number % 5)), to_float(1.0 * (number % 3)), to_float(1.0 * (number % 2))] FROM generate_series(0, 9) AS number;
CREATE TABLE test2 (value FLOAT8[], qbit FLOAT8[]);
INSERT INTO test2 (value, qbit) SELECT ARRAY[1.0, 2.0, 3.0], ARRAY[to_float(1), to_float(2), to_float(3)] FROM generate_series(1, 1);
INSERT INTO test2 (value, qbit) SELECT ARRAY[NULL::FLOAT8, 2.0, 3.0], ARRAY[to_float(1), NULL::FLOAT8, to_float(3)] FROM generate_series(1, 1);
key_bug_features: Inserting incompatible array literals into QBit column
